title: Lights Out
notes: Truly horrifying to watch, especially in complete darkness and with friends. This movie probably should move down the list because of the Diana incident and how every time Diana shows up I have to sing Diana, by One Direction, but that’s for future me to decide.

title: The Conjuring 2
notes: This movie is second on the list because of the jump scares and the fact that this movie is based off of a true story. The clips at the end of the movie from the actual investigation is terrifying. Not a great movie to watch with a group who will end up talking a lot because knowing the plot will help increase the over all viewing experience.

title: Insidious: The Last Key
notes: It is the fourth movie in the series, but it doesn’t really matter if you watch this one before the others because it technically happens before all the other movies. It’s got one of the best jump scares in it. This movie would be great to watch with people, the only problem is that since it is the fourth movie in the series, there may be a lot of questions as to who the characters are and their significance to the story. I also feel that since the ending of this movie ties to the rest of the movies, for the best watching experience, I recommend watching the first three movies.

title: The Curse of La Llorona
notes: The plot might be a little cursed and this movie is not the greatest to some people. It took me forever to actually get through this movie because of the many jump scares which is why it is placed so highly on this list. Great to watch with a group especially since there a many jump scares and the plot seems irrelevant for most of the movie.

title: Insidious
notes: Top tier movie to watch with friends. Last time I watched it, my friend had to sleep with her mom. We were seniors in high school (love her to death and yes we are still the best of friends). Soundtrack will defiantly creep out everyone too, it’s amazing.

title: The Conjuring
notes: Similar reasons to The Conjuring 2 (2016) movie, it’s terrifying listening to the real clips from the investigation at the end of the movie. The Conjuring (2013) has a few good jump scares throughout which helps with it’s ranking. It’s also impossible to ignore the fact that this is where the infamous game Hide and Clap comes from.

title: It
notes: Rule #1, never trust a clown

title: Us
notes: Love how it was filmed in Santa Cruz. It’s a good mix of funny and scary. Ending is a mindfuck, but the plot is 10/10.

title: Winchester
notes: It’s terrifying that you can literally visit the actual house. Makes it even scarier that this movie is based off of a true story. That always makes movies scarier.

title: It: Chapter 2
notes: How much more proof do you need in order to believe me when I say NEVER TRUST A CLOWN.

title: Hereditary
notes: The whole movie was kind of a blur, but there were defiantly some parts in the movie that I remember wanting to watch it with someone else. I feel it’s more gore than scary. But it does get progressively scarier.

title: Sinister
notes: Some of the clips are truly horrifying in terms of it being graphic, like people dying and stuff; also it might be a good idea to watch the first one first but its not necessary

title: A Quiet Place
notes: Very quiet

title: The Woman in Black
notes: Jump scares definitely catch you off guard. The general “check the corners and mirrors” rule doesn’t work with this movie.

title: Midsommar
notes: This movie defiantly fucks with your head. There is no monster or ghost that will jump out and kill everyone. Without spoiling much, the best way to explain it is “cult vibes”. Highly recommend this movie if you want to watch by yourself. It has an amazing plot and the acting it award-winning. Midsommar (2019) is also a great group watch movie if you’re with a group who is willing to actually watch the movie as there are lots of Easter eggs hidden throughout the movie. Lastly, the beautiful Ms. FLO stars in this film which should be reason enough to watch this movie.

title: Sinister 2
notes: This is the reason I’m scared of children. Another reason why I don’t want any.

title: The Woman in Black: Angel of Death
The Haunting of Hill House (TV show)
notes: This is a TV show on Netflix. It’s alright for a TV show. It has its moments. I really enjoyed how everything tied together as you watch the final episodes.

title: Hellraiser
notes: One word to describe this movie, kinky.

title: Together
notes: Plot was really good. Lowkey predictable but had a few good jump scares. There were some parts that had me laughing at the ridiculousness though.

title: The Ritual
notes: Cults are just scary in general. I don’t know what about cults just freak me out but it was alright.

title: The Autopsy of Jane Doe
notes: Surprised me with how scary it was. Plot was mid, but the jump scares are elite. I realise these descriptions are getting very repetitive but like lots of these movies are just average.

title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
notes: The fact that it’s based off of a true story makes this movie scarier than the movie it’s self. Yes, the movie has its moments, but still mid. It’s shocking how different this movie is compared to the other Conjuring Universe movies that are full of jump scares and terrifying moments.

title: Coraline
notes: Although this movie is animated, it terrified me as a kid and still scares people today. Love Tim Burton as a director and all of his works are incredible.

title: Gretel and Hansel
notes: Terrible plot, but kinda spooky. I don’t know what else to wright about this movie. Very, very mid.

title: The Shining
notes: Literally my favourite movie ever, no doubt about it. I realise it does not have many scary moments but it makes up for that in the amount of iconic moments it has. I believe that everyone needs to have seen this movie at least once in their lifetime. Even if you haven’t seen The Shining (1980), you have defiantly seen clips of it. Like the famous, “Here’s Johnny,” quote from Jack Nicholson. It’s a tradition that I watch this movie every Halloween. Not a great group movie if you’re with a group who are drunk or like to talk about. It is a longer movie and can get boring at some moments. Only the great can truly understand the full amazingness this movie has to offer.

title: The Invisible Man
notes: Just a man in a suit. Just saved you 2 hours of your life. Go watch a better movie.

title: The Fly
notes: Make sure when you teleport yourself, you are alone in the pod. Disgusting ass movie, but iconic.

title: The Lost Boys
notes: Classic movie I love. Again, not scary but something about purple, sparkly, vampire blood really scares my brother.

title: The Exorcist
notes: People find this extremely scary, I laughed through the entire movie.

title: Hush
notes: Good movie to watch with people who absolutely don’t wanna watch a scary movie but you do.

title: Don’t Worry Darling
notes: Harry Styles and Mrs. Flo, what more could you ask for. Messes with your head, but not many jump scares or unsettling moments.

title: You’re Next
notes: Unusually funny, especially the masks. Not really scary though.

title: Pan’s Labyrinth
notes: Watched this in Spanish and thought it was funny how everyone else was terrified of it. Tyler found this especially scary which made me laugh more.

title: Scary Stories: To Tell in the Dark
The Cabin in the Woods
notes: Was an alright movie until the end when they made it all futuristic and ruined the movie, in my opinion.

title: Doctor Sleep
notes: As much as it pains me to say it, this is the sequel to The Shining (1980) movie. I actually hate this movie so much. The first one was so good. Stephen King hinted at the possibility of ghosts and actually haunting to blame for the unexplainable events that happened up at the hotel. Then this fucking movie came out and I guess everyone has superpowers. Maybe my hopes were to high as The Shining (1980) is literally my favourite movie ever and I was expecting lots from this movie. Still, I don’t like. Watch it if you want.

title: The Mummy
notes: More action than scary, but the mummy is why this movie is on this list.

title: The Orphanage
notes: Another movie we watched in Spanish class. The only thing I remember from this movie is the kid getting hit by the car, or something like that, which set my mood for the rest of the movie.

title: Unfriended
notes: Average? It’s alright I guess. Kinda boring as it’s literally just a Zoom call the entire time. This was the first horror movie I ever watched and I see it as a gate way drug to becoming a horror fanatic, so I have to give it some respect. But not much. Would have been better as a 2020 movie. It came out a little too early.

title: American Psycho
notes: What more could you ask from a movie than a psycho killer? Good ass movie, but not scary.

title: Blair Witch
notes: Terrible. Don’t watch. There’s no point.

title: The VVITCH
notes: Again, don’t waste your time. I’ll save you the hell. Just don’t

title: Heretic
notes: Classic escape the house from a creepy, psychotic old man who gets off on killing young girls. Too bad these women are total badasses. “Knockin' on Heaven's Door”

title: Umma
notes: Sandra oh

title: Friday the 13th
notes: The filming and audio is hard to watch but it's one of the classics so I can't hate on it that much. I find it funny how dead people can blink, still trying to wrap my head around that one. I do like how you go the whole movie without knowing who the bad guy is. This is a good movie for those who really appreciate scary movies or for a movie night at summer camp.

title: Friday the 13th Part II
notes: No need to watch the first movie as the first 10 minutes of this movie is basically just a recap of everything important. The "plot" is better tho. With these old movies, it's always the dumb blonde that ends up getting killed but for once the blonde girl is the only smart one. Basically, this is the same movie as the first one. I'd say better though. So if you're going to watch one of the two, I say choose this one.

title: Friday the 13th Part III
notes: Unlike the first two movies, Part III is the finniest non-comedy horror movie. Jason finally gets his iconic hockey mask, they have sex in a hammock, and everyone consumes weed at some point (but only the potheads get high?). Anyways, this is still a classic movie in the sense that the big chase of the movie is at nigh, during a rain storm (just like the first two), the girls keep tripping over nothing, lots of screaming, and a single female survivor makes it through the night. Only thing I have to ask is what is in the Myers' blood because nothing can kill them. Watch it like a spoof, and it weirdly works.

title: Friday the 13th Part VI: Jason Lives
Friday the 13th Part VII: The New Blood
Friday the 13th Part VII: Jason Takes Manhattan
Jason Goes to Hell: The Final Friday
Halloween III: Season of the Witch
Halloween 4: The Return of Michael Myers
Halloween 5: The Revenge of Michael Myers
Halloween: The Curse of Michael Myers
Sinners
notes: Did not expect the movie to head in the direction it did at the start and even when I started to catch on, I didn't think my predictions were right. Either way, the attention to details and how music and music history was portrayed in this movie was amazing.
